text.title: FastAPI Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Sync I/O in async routes, business logic in routers, mutable default args, and missing docs_url guards.

antipattern.description: Calling synchronous I/O (requests.get, psycopg2 queries) inside async route handlers.
antipattern.reason: Sync calls block the event loop. All other pending requests wait until the blocking call returns. Use httpx.AsyncClient for HTTP and SQLAlchemy async for DB.

antipattern.description: Business logic (validation, inventory check, payment) directly in router functions.
antipattern.reason: Router functions with embedded logic cannot be tested without the HTTP stack, cannot be called from Celery tasks, and cannot be reused across endpoints.

antipattern.description: Exposing /docs and /redoc in production (docs_url="/docs" with no guard).
antipattern.reason: OpenAPI documentation exposes your full API surface, endpoint names, and schema structure. Gate with docs_url="/docs" if settings.debug else None.

antipattern.description: Reusing a single Pydantic schema for create, update, and response operations.
antipattern.reason: Create requires password; response must not expose it; update makes all fields optional. One schema cannot express all three constraints cleanly.

rule.statement: Use db.flush() + db.refresh(user) after adding a new object to get the generated ID and DB defaults without committing. Commit happens in get_db() when the request succeeds.
rule.rationale: flush() sends the INSERT to the DB without committing. refresh() reloads the object with server-generated values (UUID, created_at). The outer transaction commits or rolls back at request end.
